task_name: Live proof of first permit-gated write
goal: Record the first real permit-gated write to G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox as a verified milestone: permit issued, exactly one write succeeded, reuse blocked as already_consumed, and the Bluepilot write window closed (403 sandbox_permit_write_disabled). Document the deliberate intermediate enforcement state so a later session does not mistake the permit path for a permanent default rule. No code and no runtime change.
scope_out: Do not change any code, route, env behavior, or test.
Do not turn enforcement on or off; this task only records the observed run and required closed state.
Do not deactivate or modify the legacy /probe/sandbox-real-write path; that is a later cleanup task.
Do not touch maya-core; its anchor entry is a separate note under its own governance.
claims: review-packets/BP-147.md records permit id 5b4121c2-d991-4b9b-afc8-d16e28d31aa3, contentHash sha256:4e459ee4..., contentLen 86, target sandbox path .bluepilot/phase-3c-permit-write.md, and op create.
It records the first write as write_succeeded with commit 5327082bb0804ff1728ee39b2744fcec79d32906, externally verified to exist with an 86-byte file.
It records the reuse attempt as write_blocked with reason already_consumed and pushed false, based on the live response and prior real-Postgres consume proof.
It records the close as HTTP 403 sandbox_permit_write_disabled and names the write flags that must remain false.
It records the deliberate intermediate state: the permit path is built and live-proven once, but permanent enforcement is a later explicit decision.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md get matching entries for the live proof.
assumptions: bluepilot/main is at 289243f after the BP-146 merge.
The Bluepilot write window is closed after the test, confirmed by HTTP 403 sandbox_permit_write_disabled.
The maya-core write flags are operator-controlled Render secrets and must remain false after the test; this task documents that required closed state rather than changing it.
The reuse already_consumed result is taken from the live run output plus prior real-Postgres reproduction; it was not independently re-queried from the live register by this task.
dod: review-packets/BP-147.md exists and contains the proof facts, the external verification note, and the enforcement-intermediate-state note.
STATE.md has a BP-147 / live-proof entry.
docs/SESSION-LOG.md has a dated 2026-06-02 live-proof entry.
contracts/BP-147.json validates as doc_task with no code files in allowed_files.
stop_conditions: Any code or runtime change becomes necessary.
Touching the legacy write path becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BP-143 designed the one-shot write permit. BP-144 built the canonical contentHash and maya-core permit register plus atomic consume. BP-145 added the Bluepilot byte-output guard, UTF-8 hash before base64, and create/update GitHub semantics. BP-146 added the Bluepilot permit-write trigger and maya-core issue-write-permit script. BP-147 records the first live proof and closed state.
